name: ucdp-addr

metadata.name: ucdp-addr
metadata.summary: Unified Chip Design Platform - Address Map

documentation:
[![PyPI Version](https://badge.fury.io/py/ucdp-addr.svg)](https://badge.fury.io/py/ucdp-addr)
[![Python Build](https://github.com/nbiotcloud/ucdp-addr/actions/workflows/main.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/nbiotcloud/ucdp-addr/actions/workflows/main.yml)
[![Documentation](https://readthedocs.org/projects/ucdp-addr/badge/?version=stable)](https://ucdp-addr.readthedocs.io/en/stable/?badge=stable)
[![Coverage Status](https://coveralls.io/repos/github/nbiotcloud/ucdp-addr/badge.svg?branch=main)](https://coveralls.io/github/nbiotcloud/ucdp-addr?branch=main)
[![python-versions](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/ucdp-addr.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/ucdp-addr)

# Unified Chip Design Platform - Address Map

* [Documentation](https://ucdp-addr.readthedocs.io/en/stable/)
* [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/ucdp-addr/)
* [Sources](https://github.com/nbiotcloud/ucdp-addr)
* [Issues](https://github.com/nbiotcloud/ucdp-addr/issues)

## Installation

Installing it is pretty easy:

```bash
pip install ucdp-addr
```

class_variables.name: ucdp_addr.addrdecoder.AddrDecoder.is_sub

Address Decoder aka Demultiplexer.

An address decoder manages the access to a known list of slaves.
The address map contains the address to slave mapping.

functions.name: ucdp_addr.util.calc_depth_size
documentation:
Calc Either Depth Or Size.

```pycon
>>> calc_depth_size(32, depth=64)
(64, Bytesize('256 bytes'))
>>> calc_depth_size(32, size='64')
(16, Bytesize('64 bytes'))
>>> calc_depth_size(32, depth=64, size=256)
(64, Bytesize('256 bytes'))
>>> calc_depth_size(32)
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
ValueError: Either 'depth' or 'size' are required.
>>> calc_depth_size(32, depth=64, size='64')
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
ValueError: 'depth' and 'size' are mutally exclusive.
```

functions.name: ucdp_addr.addrspaces.join_addrspaces

functions.name: ucdp_addr.addrspaces.zip_addrspaces
documentation:
Zip Address Spaces.

```pycon
>>> one = (
...     Addrspace(name='a0', baseaddr=0x0000, size=0x1000),
...     Addrspace(name='a1', baseaddr=0x1000, size=0x1000))
>>> other = (
...     Addrspace(name='b0', baseaddr=0x0000, size=0x1000),
...     Addrspace(name='b1', baseaddr=0x1000, size=0x800),
...     Addrspace(name='b2', baseaddr=0x1800, size=0x800))
>>> for left, right in zip_addrspaces(one, other): print(repr(left.info), repr(right.info))
'a0 +0x0 1024x32' 'b0 +0x0 1024x32'
'a1 +0x1000 1024x32' 'b1 +0x1000 512x32'
'a1 +0x1000 1024x32' 'b2 +0x1800 512x32'
```

functions.name: ucdp_addr.addrspace.cast_access
documentation:
Cast Access.

Usage:

    >>> from ucdp_addr import addrspace
    >>> access = addrspace.cast_access("RO")
    >>> access
    RO
    >>> cast_access(access)
    RO

functions.name: ucdp_addr.addrspace.get_counteraccess
documentation:
Get Counter Access.

Usage:

    >>> from ucdp_addr import addrspace
    >>> str(addrspace.get_counteraccess(addrspace.RO))
    'RW'
    >>> str(addrspace.get_counteraccess(addrspace.RW))
    'RO'

methods.name: ucdp_addr.addrspace.Addrspace.is_overlapping
documentation:
Determine both Address Spaces Overlap.

    >>> one = Addrspace(name='one', baseaddr=0x2000, size='4kB')
    >>> two = Addrspace(name='two', baseaddr=0x3000, size='4kB')
    >>> three = Addrspace(name='three', baseaddr=0x2000, size='5kB')
    >>> one.is_overlapping(two)
    False
    >>> one.is_overlapping(three)
    True
    >>> three.is_overlapping(one)
    True
    >>> three.is_overlapping(two)
    True

methods.name: ucdp_addr.addrspace.Addrspace.get_intersect
documentation:
Get Intersection.

    >>> one = Addrspace(name='one', baseaddr=0x2000, size='4kB')
    >>> two = Addrspace(name='two', baseaddr=0x3000, size='4kB')
    >>> three = Addrspace(name='three', baseaddr=0x2000, size='5kB')
    >>> one.get_intersect(two)
    >>> one.get_intersect(three)
    Addrspace(name='one', baseaddr=Hex('0x2000'), size=Bytesize('4 KB'))
    >>> three.get_intersect(one)
    Addrspace(name='three', baseaddr=Hex('0x2000'), size=Bytesize('4 KB'))
    >>> three.get_intersect(two)
    Addrspace(name='three', baseaddr=Hex('0x3000'), size=Bytesize('1 KB'))
